title: pittosporum oreillyanum
intro: Pittosporum oreillyanum, also known as Australian cheese tree, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its distinctive, edible fruit and fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with its roots and leaves exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds. Consuming the fruit of the Australian cheese tree has been linked to various health benefits, including improved digestion and reduced inflammation. The leaves and roots of the plant can be prepared as infusions, tinctures, or ointments, which may be used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions and respiratory problems.
